note_data.title: Akhlaq — Islamic Morals
keyPoints: Akhlaq means moral character and conduct in Islam — it is derived from the Arabic word 'Khuluq' meaning nature or disposition
Good Akhlaq includes truthfulness (Sidq), honesty (Amanah), justice (Adl), patience (Sabr), and kindness (Husn-e-Sulook)
Bad Akhlaq includes lying (Kizb), dishonesty (Khiyanah), injustice (Zulm), anger (Ghussa), and arrogance (Takabbur)
Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) said 'I have been sent to perfect good character' — showing the importance of Akhlaq in Islam
Akhlaq with Allah includes worship, obedience, remembrance (Zikr), and avoiding shirk and sins
Akhlaq with people includes respecting parents, helping neighbors, being kind to poor, and fulfilling rights of others
Akhlaq with oneself includes cleanliness, avoiding harmful things, seeking knowledge, and self-accountability
Islamic society in Pakistan can improve through practicing good Akhlaq in daily life, business, and governance

definitions.definition: Akhlaq means moral character and conduct. It is the plural of Khuluq which means nature, temperament, or disposition.
Husn-e-Akhlaq means good moral character. It includes all praiseworthy qualities like truthfulness, honesty, justice, and kindness.
Su-e-Akhlaq means bad moral character. It includes all blameworthy qualities like lying, dishonesty, injustice, and cruelty.
Sidq means truthfulness. It is speaking the truth in all circumstances and avoiding lies.
Amanah means trustworthiness and honesty. It includes fulfilling trusts, keeping promises, and being reliable.

importantQuestions.question: Define Akhlaq and write its importance in Islam.
importantQuestions.answer: Akhlaq means moral character and conduct. It is very important in Islam because Prophet (PBUH) was sent to perfect good character. Good Akhlaq brings success in this world and hereafter.

importantQuestions.question: Explain the different types of Akhlaq with examples from Pakistani society.
importantQuestions.answer: Akhlaq has three types: (1) Akhlaq with Allah - includes worship, obedience, Zikr, avoiding shirk. Example: Praying five times daily in Pakistani mosques. (2) Akhlaq with people - includes respecting parents, helping neighbors, being kind to poor. Example: Helping flood victims in Sindh. (3) Akhlaq with oneself - includes cleanliness, avoiding harmful things, seeking knowledge. Example: Students studying hard for education in Karachi schools.

mcqs.question: Which is an example of Husn-e-Akhlaq?
Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) said 'I have been sent to perfect...'

commonMistakes: Students often confuse Akhlaq with worship only — remember it includes moral character in all aspects of life
Writing examples without Pakistani context — always use local examples like helping in floods, earthquake relief
Forgetting to define Arabic terms like Sidq, Amanah properly with exact textbook definitions
Not explaining the three types of Akhlaq clearly — with Allah, with people, with oneself
Missing Quranic verses and Hadith references that support good Akhlaq
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 long question worth 5-8 marks in BSEK papers
Memorize the exact definition of Akhlaq as it appears in STBB textbook
Always give Pakistani examples when explaining Akhlaq in society
Learn the famous Hadith about Prophet (PBUH) being sent to perfect character — appears frequently
Practice writing about all three types of Akhlaq with clear examples
note_data.keyDiagram: Draw a simple diagram showing three branches of Akhlaq: (1) Akhlaq with Allah (2) Akhlaq with People (3) Akhlaq with Oneself, with examples under each branch
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q4 (6 marks) — asked students to define Akhlaq and explain three characteristics of good moral character according to Islamic teachings
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q3 (5 marks) — required definition of Husn-ul-Khuluq and two examples from Prophet's life showing excellent character
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q6 (4 marks) — asked to differentiate between Akhlaq-e-Hasana and Akhlaq-e-Sayya with one example each
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q2 (7 marks) — demanded explanation of importance of good morals in Islamic society with Quranic references

workedExample.question: Define Akhlaq and explain any three qualities of Akhlaq-e-Hasana (Good Morals) with examples. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Write exact textbook definition of Akhlaq
Step 2: Identify three qualities of good morals from STBB textbook
Step 3: Provide one clear example for each quality
Step 4: Use proper Islamic terminology and Quranic references where mentioned in textbook
workedExample.answer: Akhlaq means moral character and conduct in Islam. It refers to the inner qualities and outward behavior of a Muslim according to Islamic teachings. Three qualities of Akhlaq-e-Hasana are: (1) Truthfulness (Sidq) - always speaking truth like Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) who was called As-Sadiq, (2) Justice (Adl) - being fair in all dealings as commanded in Quran, (3) Patience (Sabr) - remaining steadfast in difficulties as shown by Prophet Ayub (AS).
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks for correct definition of Akhlaq, 1 mark each for naming three qualities, 1 mark for relevant examples

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers about morality instead of using exact STBB textbook definitions of Akhlaq terms
Avoid mixing personal opinions with Islamic teachings - stick strictly to Quranic verses and Hadith mentioned in the textbook
Many students confuse Akhlaq-e-Hasana with Akhlaq-e-Sayya examples - memorize the exact categorization from STBB textbook
note_data.quickRecap: Akhlaq means Islamic moral character covering both inner qualities and outward behavior. Good morals (Akhlaq-e-Hasana) include truthfulness, justice, patience, and kindness as taught by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH). Bad morals (Akhlaq-e-Sayya) include lying, oppression, and arrogance which Islam strictly forbids. Moral development is essential for both individual success and Islamic society's wellbeing.
